channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: British invasion: Labour Party to descend on US to help Harris
item.description: In a now-deleted social media post, the head of operations for the British Labour Party indicated "nearly 100" current and former party staffers will be headed to the United States to help Vice President Kamala Harris during the final stretch before the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Submit your questions for the JD Vance town hall on NewsNation
item.description: X users can use the hashtag #VanceTownHall to submit questions to JD Vance, moderated by Chris Cuomo.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump earned money from government sources through Washington hotel, Democrats find
item.description: Over 11 months while former President Trump was in office, his Washington, D.C., hotel took in some $300,000 from sources inside and outside of government, including tens of thousands in payments from the U.S. Secret Service.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abortion is on the ballot in 10 states. Here's where the measures stand
item.description: Voters in 10 states will decide this November whether to adopt constitutional amendments that could shape the future of abortion access in their states.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Senate hopefuls tackle trans athletes, immigration and UFOs in only debate
item.description: Sen. Jackie Rosen and Republican candidate Sam Brown went head to head in the Nevada Senate debate on immigration, the economy and biological males competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What's a 'Jezebel spirit'? Some Christians use the term to paint Kamala Harris with a demonic brush
item.description: Christian nationalist leaders are telling followers that Vice President Kamala Harris is under the influence of a “Jezebel spirit.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert And Michelle R. Smith, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Republicans mainstreamed the baseless idea of noncitizen voting in 2024
item.description: Donald Trump and his allies have zeroed in on the baseless claim that Democrats are encouraging newly arrived migrants to vote for them in the 2024 election. There is no evidence of a plot like this.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Miles Parks, MPR News

item.title: Democrats look to quash threat from Green Party's Jill Stein
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over Green Party candidate Jill Stein as they look to avoid a repeat of the 2016 presidential election, in which Stein was accused of playing spoiler in key swing states. For months, Democrats had trained their ire on Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hovde faces high-stakes moment in bid to unseat Democrat in Wisconsin Senate race
item.description: Republican Eric Hovde is facing a critical moment in Friday's first Wisconsin Senate debate amid signs he's closing in on Democrat Sen. Tammy Baldwin in one of the country's most competitive races for the upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris holds 1-point lead over Trump nationally: poll
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris leads former President Trump by one point nationally, according to an Emerson College survey released on Friday. The national survey found 49 percent of voters said they supported Harris while 48 percent said they backed Trump.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Crunch time: Harris to team up with Barack and Michelle Obama next week in key battlegrounds
item.description: As the 2024 election showdown between Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Trump reaches the home stretch, Harris is teaming up with Barack and Michelle Obama in key battleground states
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As campaign nears finish, Walz adds dashes of digs to his portions of joy
item.description: The Democratic vice presidential candidate entered the race emphasizing positivity in politics. In the final stretch to Election Day, he’s trying to thread the needle between needling Donald Trump and JD Vance while still keeping an optimistic message.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Biden says Western allies must keep aiding Ukraine as he meets European partners before election
item.description: U.S. President Joe Biden has said as he meets with European partners that it’s important for Ukraine’s Western allies to “sustain our resolve” in supporting the country.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris and Trump target Michigan as both parties try to shore up 'blue wall' votes
item.description: Kamala Harris and Donald Trump will both be scouring for votes in Michigan on Friday as they try to lock down support in this key political battleground.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump delivers a pointed and at times bitter speech at Al Smith charity dinner
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former President Donald Trump laced into Vice President Kamala Harris and other Democrats on Thursday in a pointed and at times bitter speech as he headlined the annual Al Smith charity dinner in New York.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Harris keeps a focus on 'blue wall' states in the campaign's final weeks
item.description: With three weeks left in the presidential campaign, Democrat Kamala Harris is spending most of her days trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin as she tries to avoid a repeat of Hillary Clinton’s collapse there eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Return of 'candidate chaos'? Why some voters may see Vince Fong twice on their ballot
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- On Nov. 5, Congressman Vince Fong is hoping to lose -- well, in one of two races. That's because for some voters, his name will be on the ballot twice: once for Congress and once for State Assembly. Fong continues to emphasize he's only running for Congress.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At Wisconsin stops, Harris campaign features less talk of joy and more attacks on Trump
item.description: In La Crosse on Thursday, Harris told a rally crowd that Trump “will stop at nothing to claim unchecked power for himself.” It’s a shift from the “joy” that swirled around her elevation to the top of the Democratic ticket this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Republicans appeal a Georgia judge’s ruling that invalidates seven election rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — National and state Republicans on Thursday appealed a judge's ruling that said seven election rules recently passed by Georgia's State Election Board are “illegal, unconstitutional and void.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris campaign features less talk of joy and more head-on digs at Trump as Election Day nears
item.description: LA CROSSE, Wis. (AP) — Joy Olson proudly wore a “Make America Joyful Again” button Thursday as she waited in line to attend a Kamala Harris rally.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From the 'Shark Tank' to the campaign trail: Mark Cuban teams up with Kamala Harris
item.description: Billionaire businessman Mark Cuban steps up his campaign efforts for Vice President Kamala Harris - with stops in battleground Wisconsin - as she faces off against former President Trump
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump visits barbershop in the Bronx
item.description: THE BRONX, N.Y. (PIX11) – Former President Donald Trump visited a barbershop in the Bronx on Thursday. Trump stopped by Javiel's Barbershop on Castle Hill Avenue.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Key Pennsylvania region sees ‘waitlist’ for Trump signs; lawmaker says voters ready to reverse Dem policies
item.description: Lehigh County Republican State Sen. Jarrett Coleman and Northampton County GOP leaders Andrew Azan III and Tom Carroll spoke to Fox News Digital about their collective ground game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 25 Pinellas County voting locations change after hurricanes
item.description: Twenty-five polling locations in Pinellas County have been changed due to hurricanes Helene and Milton, according to the county supervisor of elections.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance speculates why Pennsylvania is trending red, warns battleground not to trust Harris on manufacturing
item.description: Vice presidential candidate Sen. JD Vance, R-Ohio, credits former President Trump's tariff proposal to revive American manufacturing as driving GOP support in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tried a friendlier pitch with Latino voters at a Univision town hall
item.description: During a town hall outside Miami, Trump touted his record on the economy, yet called Jan. 6 a "day of love" and would not back off false claims about Haitian migrants eating pets in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Lorena Biasotti, CCSD school board candidate, discusses priorities ahead of election
item.description: Lorena Biasotti, a mother of four and candidate for Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, spoke about her platform on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mayor Johnson's chief of staff says school CEO and board kept 5th Floor in the dark
item.description: In an exclusive sitdown, Cristina Pacione-Zayas invited WGN to City Hall’s 5th floor to, in her words, “clear the air.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Trump focuses on women’s issues in front of all-women audience in Fox News town hall
item.description: The former president took questions on immigration, the economy and abortion in the hour-long town hall in front of a friendly crowd of women in suburban Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Republican Party sues Hennepin County over election judges list
item.description: A state law requires each county in Minnesota select election judges from lists provided by the Republican and Democratic parties. A lawsuit claims Hennepin County violated state statute by not selecting judges from a GOP list.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Nevada political parties seek to sway ‘Gen Z’ amid inflation and recovery from an on-campus mass shooting
item.description: Young Democrats and College Republicans at UNLV are campaigning for their respective candidates in the 2024 election, citing issues such as inflation, gun violence, and reproductive freedoms, while the Trump campaign is targeting students through social media and face-to-face meetings.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans grapple with storm of misinformation from their own party
item.description: Republicans representing states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton are grappling with the spread of storm misinformation, much of which has stemmed from prominent figures in their own party — including sitting lawmakers and former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Rufus Wainwright blasts Trump playing 'Hallelujah' at town hall
item.description: The 51-year-old singer said Tuesday that he was "horrified" to learn his 2001 cover of Leonard Cohen's "Hallelujah" was part of the playlist at the Trump campaign event a night earlier.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Control of the Minnesota House is up for grabs this fall. Here are 14 races to watch
item.description: While all seats in the Minnesota House of Representatives are on the ballot this fall, just a fraction are taking up the focus and much of the fundraising for the major parties.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Question 3: 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide
item.description: Dr. Sandra Cosgrove and Kerry Durmick joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to provide a proponent and opponent's view of one of the most contentious issues on the Nevada ballot in November: Question 3.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump uses interview on economics to promote tariffs and riff on his favorite themes
item.description: Donald Trump has seized on an opening to sound his frequent argument that imposing huge tariffs on foreign goods would amount to an economic elixir — one he claims would raise enormous sums for the government, protect U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
